task_completion.visual_evidence: Browser showing the JFrog Artifactory login page or dashboard at http://localhost:8082.

task_completion.description: Log into JFrog Artifactory using the provided credentials
task_completion.visual_evidence: The JFrog Artifactory interface is visible in an authenticated state (e.g., admin profile visible, dashboard accessible).

task_completion.description: Navigate to the 'example-repo-local' repository in the Artifacts section
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Artifacts Tree or Repository Browser is open in the UI, showing the 'example-repo-local' repository.

task_completion.description: Locate the specific artifact within the repository path
task_completion.visual_evidence: The repository tree is expanded to 'org/apache/commons/commons-lang3/3.14.0' and the file 'commons-lang3-3.14.0.jar' is visible and/or selected.

task_completion.description: Initiate the download of the artifact
task_completion.visual_evidence: The agent clicks the 'Download' button in the Artifactory UI for the selected jar file, or a browser download prompt/progress bar appears.

task_completion.description: Save the artifact to the expected download path
task_completion.visual_evidence: Browser download history, a file manager window, or terminal output showing 'commons-lang3-3.14.0.jar' successfully saved in the '/home/ga/Downloads/' directory.

integrity.description: The agent must download the file from Artifactory, not copy the local source file.
integrity.visual_evidence: Ensure there are no terminal commands like 'cp /home/ga/artifacts/... /home/ga/Downloads/...' used to bypass the actual download process.

integrity.description: The agent interacted with the Artifactory interface or used a valid HTTP request to download the file.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show the browser interacting with the Artifactory UI or a valid curl/wget command targeting localhost:8082, rather than just creating a dummy file with the touch command or a text editor.

integrity.description: The downloaded file has the exact correct name and extension.
integrity.visual_evidence: The file in the Downloads folder must be named exactly 'commons-lang3-3.14.0.jar' without any extra extensions like '.txt' or browser duplication suffixes like '(1)'.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The target artifact is 'commons-lang3-3.14.0.jar'. It is located in the JFrog Artifactory at 'example-repo-local/org/apache/commons/commons-lang3/3.14.0/'. The expected download path is '/home/ga/Downloads/commons-lang3-3.14.0.jar'. Note: The source file originally resides at '/home/ga/artifacts/commons-lang3/commons-lang3-3.14.0.jar' - the agent must download it from Artifactory, not just copy this local source file.
